title: Gravity Well Sandbox
description: An interactive 2D Newtonian gravity simulation where users click-and-drag to launch planets into orbit around a central star, with real-time physics, fading orbital trails, impact ring effects, and parameter modulation.
author: Girish Madarkar
features: Newtonian inverse-square kinematics: a = G·M/r² per axis, integrated via Euler step v += a, x += v each rAF frame

Click-and-drag launcher: mousedown anchors spawn point, mousemove draws dashed vector with arrowhead, mouseup instantiates planet with velocity proportional to drag length × 0.04 in opposite direction
Circular collision absorption: distance < star.r + planet.r check removes planet and spawns expanding ring with alpha fade (life -= 0.025, r += 0.5)
Out-of-bounds pruning: planets beyond canvas ±200px deleted to prevent memory leak
Fading orbital trails: per-planet 50-120 position history buffer drawn as progressive-alpha line segments (j/tr.length × 0.6) with emerald glow
Parameter modulation: G slider (100-3000), Mass slider (500-8000), trail length slider (10-120), checkbox toggle for trails on/off
Impact ring effect: expanding circle stroke with globalAlpha fade, drawn as expanding ring micro-animation per absorption event
Telemetry panel: satellite count, peak velocity tracker, relative coordinates from star center
Resize handler: re-centers star at new midpoint, re-scales star radius proportional to W × 0.025
Touch support: touchstart/touchmove/touchend with preventDefault for mobile launch interaction
